reasoning: Shantanu Thakur pushed for the CAA when my father needed it most, and it became law because of the 2019 vote for Modi. The notification came—slowly, yes, and my father is still waiting two months after submitting his application, and those fourteen certificates five days before polling feel like theater, not deliverance. But Biswajit Das is a political weathervane, and TMC had years to prevent Sandeshkhali instead of letting strongmen like Shahjahan run things. I am not voting to reward TMC or punish it; I am voting because my father's legal existence still matters more than the ration card. Thakur started this, and I want him to finish it, even if the finishing is taking longer than I hoped.
primary_drivers: CAA and father's legal status (even though implementation is disappointingly slow)
Shantanu Thakur's personal role in pushing for the law
TMC's failures (Sandeshkhali, SSC scam, Biswajit Das as a weak and unreliable candidate)
